skill.name: nx-workspace-patterns
skill.description: Configure and optimize Nx monorepo workspaces. Use when setting up Nx, configuring project boundaries, optimizing build caching, or implementing affected commands.
skill.summary: Production-ready patterns for Nx monorepo management including project configuration, module boundaries, and CI optimization.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 35 patterns that are all false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only content containing Nx configuration templates, CLI command examples, and best practices. External command patterns are documentation code blocks, network references are hyperlinks to official Nx documentation, and filesystem patterns are JSON schema paths in configuration templates. No executable code or security risks present.

content.value_statement: Managing large-scale JavaScript projects becomes complex without proper tooling. This skill provides production-ready Nx workspace patterns for efficient monorepo management with build caching and affected commands.
actual_capabilities: Generate Nx workspace configuration templates with proper task runner options
Configure project boundaries using ESLint module boundary rules
Set up remote caching with Nx Cloud or self-hosted S3 solutions
Create CI workflows using affected commands for efficient builds
Design custom generators for consistent library scaffolding
Optimize build performance through proper cache configuration
limitations: Does not execute Nx commands directly - provides configuration guidance only
Templates target Nx with React - may need adaptation for other frameworks
Remote caching setup requires external Nx Cloud account or AWS infrastructure
Does not cover migration strategies from other monorepo tools

use_cases.title: New Nx Workspace Setup
use_cases.description: Configure a new Nx monorepo with proper project structure, caching, and task runner options for a team starting a large-scale React application.

use_cases.title: CI Pipeline Optimization
use_cases.description: Implement affected commands in GitHub Actions to reduce CI times by only testing and building changed projects.

use_cases.title: Module Boundary Enforcement
use_cases.description: Set up ESLint rules to enforce architectural boundaries between feature, UI, and utility libraries across the organization.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Workspace Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new Nx workspace for a React application with proper caching configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Project Boundary Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate ESLint module boundary rules for an Nx workspace with apps and libs directories, enforcing that apps can depend on features and utilities but features cannot depend on other features.

prompt_templates.title: CI Workflow with Affected Commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that uses Nx affected commands to run lint, test, and build only on changed projects when pulling from main branch.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Library Generator
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a custom Nx generator that creates a feature library with pre-configured tags, index exports, and barrel files for a scoped monorepo structure.

output_examples.input: Configure nx.json with caching for build, test, and lint operations
output_examples.output: Provides complete nx.json configuration with tasksRunnerOptions, targetDefaults, namedInputs, and cacheable operations for optimal CI performance.

output_examples.input: Set up module boundaries preventing circular dependencies
output_examples.output: Generates ESLint configuration with depConstraints enforcing tag-based dependency rules between apps, features, UI libraries, and utilities.

best_practices: Use descriptive tags consistently and enforce them with module boundary rules
Enable build caching early in project setup for significant CI time savings
Keep libraries focused with single responsibility to maintain clean dependencies
anti_patterns: Creating circular dependencies between libraries which breaks the dependency graph
Skipping affected commands and running full test suites on every commit
Over-granularizing with too many small libraries increasing maintenance burden

faq.question: What is the difference between Nx affected and running all tasks?
faq.answer: Affected commands only run tasks on projects that changed since a base commit, significantly reducing CI times compared to running on all projects.

faq.question: How does Nx caching work?
faq.answer: Nx caches task outputs based on inputs like source files and configuration. When inputs match a previous run, Nx restores cached results instead of re-executing.

faq.question: What are module boundaries and why use them?
faq.answer: Module boundaries are ESLint rules that enforce architectural constraints, preventing improper dependencies between library types and scopes.

faq.question: Do I need Nx Cloud for caching?
faq.answer: No, local caching works without Nx Cloud. Nx Cloud enables distributed caching across team members and CI runners for additional speed improvements.

faq.question: How do I migrate an existing workspace to Nx?
faq.answer: Use nx migrate commands to add Nx to existing projects. Start with adding nx package, then convert package.json scripts to Nx targets gradually.

faq.question: What library types should I use in my Nx workspace?
faq.answer: Common types include feature for business logic, ui for presentational components, data-access for API integration, util for pure functions, and shell for app bootstrapping.
